title: Oggem Baobab
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Oggem.png]] |name=Oggem Baobab |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]], [[Roon/Legends|Roon]]{{PolyhedronCite|170|Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO}} establishes that ''[[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO]]'', including Oggem Baobab's [[Death/Legends|death]] on [[Roon/Legends|Roon]] in "[[Across the Roon Sea]]," takes place fifteen [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior to the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which corresponds to [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. Since Oggem Baobab arrived on [[Roon/Legends|Roon]] sixty years prior to the series' events, he must have done so in [[75 BBY]]. |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]{{DroidsCite|Across the Roon Sea}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him{{DB|character|mungobaobab|Baobab, Mungo}} |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|White]] |feathers= |eyes=Brown |skin=Light |cyber= |families=[[Baobab family]] |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|You're my great-uncle Oggem? You left home 60 years ago on an expedition to find Roonstones.''"
"''Yes. When this ship crashed, I was marooned here. And I found Roonstones. Six bags full.|Mungo and Oggem Baobab|}} '''Oggem Baobab''', known locally as "'''Old Ogger'''," was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Prospector/Legends|prospector]] and a member of the [[Baobab family]]. In [[75 BBY]], he traveled to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Roon/Legends|Roon]], searching for [[Roonstone]]s. While he did find some—six bags' worth—his [[Agricultural freighter|freighter]] crashed and caused him to be marooned. He continued his quest to no avail, looking for the stones' source for the next sixty [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. In [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]], when his [[Death/Legends|end]] was near, he was visited by his great-nephew, [[Mungo Baobab]], to whom he gave his tools and asked to continue the search. Mungo accepted but ultimately prioritized saving his friends over the search, per his uncle's final advice to him. Eventually, Mungo found the source, but it was destroyed in a [[Mission to Tawntoom|skirmish]] with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. ==Biography== ===Searching for Roonstones=== {{Quote|How I longed for someone to share this dream with. And now you've come.|Oggem Baobab, to his great-nephew|}} Oggem Baobab was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Prospector/Legends|prospector]] of the [[Baobab family]], a respected clan [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-2|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 2|nolive=1}} [[Manda/Legends|Manda]]. In [[75 BBY]], Baobab traveled on an expedition to the planet [[Roon/Legends|Roon]] in an obsessive search for the source of valuable [[Roonstone]]s. While he did find the stones on Roon, he crashed his [[agricultural freighter]] and was marooned in an area known as the [[Bantha Graveyard (Roon)|Bantha Graveyard]].''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' He was thought to have vanished. While stuck on Roon, he was able to fill six bags with Roonstones, but he never found their original source, despite coming close. [[File:OggemMungo.png|thumb|left|275px|Mungo meets his great-uncle, Old Ogger.]] Sixty [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later in [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]], Baobab was still stuck in his [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]], and [[Death/Legends|death]] was imminent for him. By then, some believed the freighter to be haunted. Oggem's great-nephew, [[Mungo Baobab]], had also taken up the search for Roonstones and traveled to Oggem's [[Starship/Legends|starship]], where he met the old man. Oggem greeted his nephew along with his companions, one of whom was [[Auren Yomm Baobab|Auren Yomm]], an inhabitant of Roon. Yomm recognized him as "Old Ogger," whom Mungo's companions only knew as a local [[Hermit/Legends|hermit]]. Mungo tried to ask his uncle about the Roonstones, but Oggem required him to come close to him due to his poor [[Eye/Legends|eyesight]]. Once he did, Oggem noticed that he wore the [[Clothing/Legends|uniform]] of the [[Baobab Merchant Fleet]]. Mungo then introduced himself, surprised that Oggem had recognized the [[Fleet/Legends|fleet's]] [[Color/Legends|colors]]. Oggem gave his name, which Mungo recognized, remembering that his great-uncle had gone on an expedition for Roonstones sixty years prior. Oggem told his story of the ship crashing and the Roonstones which he had found, happy to finally be able to share his dream with someone else. Knowing that his death would come soon, he gave his equipment and six bags of Roonstones to Mungo, asking him to finish his life's work of finding the stones' source. He then uttered one final message, reminding Mungo to never prioritize treasure over friends and family, a lesson that he himself had learned. He then died of his old age, with his nephew inviting him to rest well. ===Legacy=== {{Quote|I remembered what Uncle Oggem said. Friends are important.|Mungo Baobab|}} [[File:OggemEquipment.png|thumb|right|250px|Mungo continued to follow his great-uncle's advice and use his equipment after his death.]] Mungo, Yomm, and their two [[Droid/Legends|droids]], [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], then left the ship and went outside to examine what Oggem had left for Mungo. Inside Oggem's pouch, they found a [[memling]], a stretchy material used for many purposes; a [[seed launcher]], a type of [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]; and a small pyramid that Mungo theorized to be a clue to the Roonstones' source. He wished to follow the clue in order to follow his uncle's final wish and, although Yomm asked him to reconsider, he did not listen. Later that [[Standard day/Legends|day]], after Mungo was led to the [[Roon Sea]] by the clues to the Roonstones, his [[Boat/Legends|boat]] was caught in a whirlpool. In order to [[Skirmish on the Roon Sea|save]] him and his friends, he was forced to dump the six bags of Roonstones, following his uncle's final advice to place his friends first. The advice continued to influence Mungo further on when he noticed that he valued saving Yomm from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Bisad Koong]] over the Roonstone quest. Even after all the Roonstones were destroyed in a [[Mission to Tawntoom|fight]] against the Empire, Mungo was happy, as he had met Yomm in the process.{{DroidsCite|The Frozen Citadel}} ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|My eyes aren't what they used to be.|Oggem Baobab|}} Oggem Baobab was obsessed with his Roonstone search, continuing it for sixty years. His final advice to his great-nephew was to always place high importance on friendship and value it over treasure. He had white [[Hair/Legends|hair]], light skin, and brown eyes that had trouble seeing in his old age. ==Equipment== {{Quote|My kit. You continue the search, Mungo.|Oggem Baobab gifts his kit to Mungo|}} Oggem utilized a freighter and continued to live in it after it crashed. He owned a [[Seed/Legends|seed]] launcher and memling, both of which he gave to Mungo so that he could continue the search for the Roonstones. ==Behind the scenes== Oggem Baobab first appeared in "[[Across the Roon Sea]]," the twelfth episode of the animated series [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']], which aired on [[November 23]], [[1985]].{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}} "[[Saturday Morning "Star Wars"|Saturday Morning 'Star Wars']]"—a magazine article published in [[Starlog (magazine)|''Starlog''{{'s}}]] third yearbook volume in [[1988]]—mistakenly spells Baobab's name as "Oggam."{{Starlog|Yearbook Vol. 3|Saturday Morning "Star Wars"|Saturday Morning 'Star Wars'}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Oggem Baobab}} *{{DroidsCite|Across the Roon Sea}} {{1st}} *{{DroidsCite|The Frozen Citadel}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|mungobaobab|Baobab, Mungo}} *{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-2|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 2|nolive=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |nl=Oggem Baobab }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Baobab, Oggem}} [[Category:Baobab family]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Miners]] [[Category:Roon residents]] [[Category:Space explorers]]
